fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: DC Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in District of Columbia — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license district of columbia, dental assistant certification district of columbia, RDA district of columbia, DANB CDA district of columbia, dental assistant radiography district of columbia

board.name: DC Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: DC Health Licensing Division, 899 North Capitol Street NE, Washington, DC 20002

license_types.title: Licensed Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in District of Columbia.

steps.title: Create an Account and Apply Online
steps.description: Register for an account on the DOH Licensure Online Services Portal, complete the online application for Dental Assistant licensure, and pay the required fee.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Schedule and undergo a criminal background check (CBC) through an approved vendor as specified on the DC Health website. Results must be submitted directly to the Board.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload scanned copies of all necessary documents through the DOH Licensure Online Services Portal. This includes: a valid government-issued photo ID, proof of completion from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program (with official transcripts) or current certification from the Dental Assisting National Board (DANB), a high school diploma or GED, current Basic Life Support (BLS) or Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) certification for healthcare providers, and a passport-style photograph.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application via the DOH Licensure Online Services Portal. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Board of Dentistry to avoid delays.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the DC Board of Dentistry by license.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, District of Columbia requires Licensed Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in District of Columbia.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $190. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Licensed Dental Assistant through the DC Board of Dentistry by license.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in District of Columbia
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant license in DC, applicants must register and submit their application through the DOH Licensure Online Services Portal.

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to the DOH Licensure Online Services Portal.
2. Click "Register here" to create a new account.
3. Complete the registration form with your personal details and create login credentials.
4. Verify your email address as instructed.
5. Log in with your new credentials to access the application system.
